title: new ApolloServer with csrfPrevention: false — the GraphQL API accepts simple-request CSRF

params.passDetail: ApolloServer keeps CSRF prevention on (csrfPrevention is not false) — mutations require a preflighted request, blocking simple-request cross-site forgery.
params.failDetail: new ApolloServer is created with csrfPrevention: false, disabling the check that requires a non-simple (preflighted) request. A malicious page can now trigger state-changing GraphQL operations using the victim's cookies via a simple cross-site request (CSRF) — no token, no preflight. Leave csrfPrevention enabled (the Apollo Server 4 default).
params.absentCallDetail: No ApolloServer construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: ApolloServer does not set csrfPrevention; the secure default (enabled) applies.

provenance.note: csrfPrevention: false is set to unblock a non-standard client that sends simple requests, but it reopens CSRF on every mutation. The API keeps working, so the disabled protection goes unnoticed.
